create_app.description: Factory function returning configured FastAPI application with CORS, exception handlers, and lifespan management

get_engine.description: Returns SQLite engine configured with WAL mode, busy_timeout, and connection pooling

get_session.description: FastAPI dependency yielding a SQLModel session for request-scoped database access

run_migrations.description: Runs Alembic migrations to head on application startup

SyncEngine.description: Bidirectional sync between SQLite and .rapid-web/ directory. Methods: sync_to_disk(), sync_from_disk(), full_sync()

health_endpoint.signature: GET /api/health -> {status: str, version: str, uptime: float}
health_endpoint.description: Health check endpoint for service liveness verification

structured_logger.description: Returns logger with JSON formatter and rotation, bound to service context

systemd_unit_template.description: Template systemd unit file for ~/.config/systemd/user/ with linger support

sqlite_wal_mode.description: SQLite must use WAL journal mode with busy_timeout=5000ms to handle concurrent reads

idle_resource_budget.description: Service must use <50 MB RAM and <0.1% CPU when idle

graceful_shutdown.description: Service handles SIGTERM gracefully, closing database connections and completing in-flight requests

definition.scope: Foundational Python backend service: FastAPI application shell, SQLite database with Alembic migrations, systemd/launchd service registration, structured logging, health endpoints, and the hybrid data sync engine.

tasks.description: Create FastAPI application factory with CORS, exception handlers, lifespan events
Set up SQLite with SQLModel, WAL mode, and Alembic migration infrastructure
tasks.acceptance: Migrations run on startup, schema is versioned

tasks.description: Implement SyncEngine for bidirectional SQLite <-> .rapid-web/ synchronization
tasks.acceptance: Changes in either store propagate to the other

tasks.description: Create structured JSON logging with rotation
tasks.acceptance: Logs written to ~/.rapid/logs/ in JSON format

tasks.description: Create systemd user unit and launchd plist templates
tasks.acceptance: Service starts on boot when installed

acceptance: FastAPI service starts on port 8998 bound to 127.0.0.1
SQLite database created at ~/.rapid/rapid.db with WAL mode
Alembic migrations run automatically on startup
Service template files generated for systemd and launchd
